Hawaii · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology under the Hawaii Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs (DCCA). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$175
$25 application + $150 license fee
Renewal
$120
Every 2 years (odd years)
Processing Time
4 to 8 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 3469, Honolulu, HI 96801

Physical Address

335 Merchant St., Room 301, Honolulu, HI 96813

Phone & Fax

Phone: (808) 586-3000

Fax: (808) 586-2689

Email

pvl@dcca.hawaii.gov

Credential Categories

Hawaii SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Hawaii.

Provisional SLP License

Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Hawaii Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination (test code 5331, score 162).

Complete Clinical Fellowship

Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

Submit Application to DCCA

Submit application with fees to DCCA Professional and Vocational Licensing Division.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF completion documentation.
  • Background checkCriminal background check.
Telepractice

Hawaii Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Hawaii permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. Hawaii is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. A valid Hawaii SLP license is required to treat clients in the state.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Hawaii issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Hawaii does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Hawaii licensure.

Frequently Asked Questions

Hawaii SLP FAQ

How much does a Hawaii SLP license cost?

The total initial cost is approximately $175. Biennial renewal is $120.

Is Hawaii part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

No. Hawaii has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

Does Hawaii allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes. Hawaii permits telepractice but requires a valid Hawaii SLP license.
